feedback.markdownBody:
# Verdict: APPROVED

## Summary
PR fixes typecheck error in `packages/contracts/src/editor.ts` by switching from `Schema.Literal(...EDITOR_IDS)` to `Schema.Literals(EDITOR_IDS)` with an explicit typed tuple. PAN-951 feature requirements (`pan resources` command, orphan detection, `--json` flag, skill file) were already implemented on `main` (commit `0374fa0ca`). This PR unblocks the verification gate. Separate typecheck errors in `src/cli/commands/open.ts` may need follow-up if they still block verification after merge.

## Blockers (MUST fix before merge)

_none_

## High Priority (SHOULD fix; synthesis may still approve if justified)

_none_

## Nits (advisory — safe to defer)

- `packages/contracts/src/editor.ts:18` — `?` — Hardcoded `EDITOR_IDS` can drift from `EDITORS`. Derive from `EDITORS.map((e) => e.id) as [EditorId, ...EditorId[]]` to preserve single source of truth. (correctness)

## Cross-cutting groups

_none_

## What's good
- Clean, minimal fix — one file, one schema construction change.
- Security reviewer confirmed no new attack surface introduced.
- Requirements reviewer verified typecheck passes and all PAN-951 deliverables are satisfied.

## Review stats
- Blockers: 0   High: 0   Medium: 0   Nits: 1
- By reviewer: correctness=1, security=0, performance=0, requirements=0
- Files touched: 1   Files with findings: 1

## Appendix: individual reviews

See individual reviewer output files listed in `## Reviewer Output Files` in the Synthesis Context above. Those files contain full per-reviewer detail; this synthesis is the policy layer.

## ✅ CODE APPROVED — YOUR WORK IS COMPLETE

**Do NOT make any more changes.**
**Do NOT run `pan done` again.**
**Do NOT run `pan review request`.**

The specialist pipeline will now run tests. If tests pass, the issue enters the merge queue for human approval.

# Review: COMMENTED

## Summary

Review did not produce synthesis output
